2023-01-01.title: In Bucha, a Final Rampage Served as a Coda to a Month of Atrocities
2023-01-01.abstract: Hours before Russian troops began withdrawing from the suburban town, a Russian soldier left a trail of blood and devastated lives in a last paroxysm of violence.

2023-01-01.title: North Korea Vows to Escalate Nuclear Threat Against the South
2023-01-01.abstract: Kim Jong-un told a Workers’ Party meeting that his country must mass-produce nuclear missiles that could hit South Korea.

2023-01-01.title: Putin Has a New Year’s Message. So Do His Critics, From Jail.
2023-01-01.abstract: Seasonal greetings from imprisoned opponents offered counterprogramming of a sort to the traditional end-of-year speech from President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia.

2023-01-01.title: Lula Becomes Brazil’s President, With Bolsonaro in Florida
2023-01-01.abstract: Brazil inaugurates its new president, Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva, on Sunday. Facing investigations, former President Jair Bolsonaro has taken refuge in Orlando.

2023-01-01.title: Russian Airstrikes Leave Ukrainians Few Options but to Endure
2023-01-01.abstract: Ten months into the war, Ukraine has turned the tide in ground combat, but its cities are still facing relentless waves of missiles and exploding drones.

2023-01-02.title: Climate Activists Find a Way to Get Germany’s Attention: Stop Traffic
2023-01-02.abstract: Extreme tactics have pushed the climate crisis to the top of the public discourse but also stirred debate over whether the activists are going too far.

2023-01-02.title: ‘Biofoul’ on Hull Strands Cruise Ship at Sea for 6 Days
2023-01-02.abstract: An unidentified organism prompted Australia to block the vessel from docking, forcing passengers to skip a series of port stops.

2023-01-02.title: 4 Dead in Helicopter Collision in Australia
2023-01-02.abstract: The two craft crashed in midair in a tourist area on the Gold Coast, near a theme park. An investigation is underway, with a report expected to take weeks.

2023-01-02.title: ‘I Hold Him in My Heart’: Tens of Thousands Pay Respects to Benedict
2023-01-02.abstract: The pope emeritus is lying in state in St. Peter’s Basilica, where some 65,000 mourners, including Roman Catholic faithful and tourists, had paid last respects by Monday evening.

2023-01-02.title: Amid Fresh Wave of U.K. Strikes, Support for Many Walkouts Remains High
2023-01-02.abstract: Public sympathy for striking nurses and other health workers is particularly strong, posing a challenge for Prime Minister Rishi Sunak, who has promised to confront trade unions.

2023-01-03.title: In Romania, U.S. Troops Train Close to Russia’s War, in Signal to Moscow
2023-01-03.abstract: The deployment to a NATO country near the conflict in Ukraine is seen as a model of deterrence for a U.S. military that has recently stepped back from direct fighting.

2023-01-03.title: Hard-Line Israeli Minister Visits Volatile Jerusalem Holy Site
2023-01-03.abstract: Itamar Ben-Gvir, the ultranationalist national security minister, toured the site just days after taking office, drawing a furious reaction from Palestinian leaders.

2023-01-03.title: At Least 51 Dead in the Philippines After Holiday Flooding
2023-01-03.abstract: Heavy rains on Christmas Day killed dozens of people and left thousands displaced. For a deeply Catholic nation, the timing heightened the sense of tragedy.

2023-01-03.title: Deadly strike on Russians in Ukraine exposes Moscow’s military failings.

2023-01-03.title: China Denounces Covid Testing Rules Imposed on Its Travelers
2023-01-03.abstract: A government spokeswoman said Beijing would consider reciprocal measures against countries that have sought to limit travelers arriving from China.

2023-01-04.title: Ukraine Keeps Downing Russian Drones, but Price Tag Is High

2023-01-04.title: Benedict Leaves Behind a Conflicted Legacy on Clerical Sexual Abuse
2023-01-04.abstract: Joseph Ratzinger was accused of mishandling cases when he was bishop of Munich, but as pope he was credited with forcing the Catholic Church to face a scourge long ignored.

2023-01-04.title: Russia says soldiers’ cellphone use led to the deadly Makiivka strike.

2023-01-04.title: ​Is Kim Jong-un’s ‘Most Beloved Daughter’ North Korea’s Next Leader?
2023-01-04.abstract: Her appearances in state media, most recently in undated photos released on New Year’s Day, have triggered growing speculation about succession plans in the country.

2023-01-04.title: A House Divided
2023-01-04.abstract: House Republicans are struggling with a test of governing.

2023-01-05.abstract: Investigators have uncovered widespread evidence of abuse by Moscow’s forces, including rape and forced nudity. Kremlin officials have repeatedly denied accusations of rights violations.

2023-01-05.abstract: Pope Francis’ short speech honored the former pontiff, whose life’s work was meditating on the life of Jesus, and who faced many challenges before retiring in 2013.

2023-01-05.abstract: “He knocked me to the floor,” Harry recounts of the alleged assault by Prince William, according to The Guardian, which said it had obtained a copy of Harry’s coming book.

2023-01-06.abstract: After 10 months of Russian destruction, Ukraine’s economy shrank by 30 percent. But companies have packed up and moved, switched products and found support abroad.

2023-01-06.title: Can Australia Escape a Recession?

2023-01-06.abstract: In Mexico, friends, families and colleagues get together on Jan. 6, Three Kings Day, to share rosca de reyes and hot chocolate or atole, a Mexican beverage.

2023-01-06.abstract: Millions are expected to travel home this month, spreading Covid to rural communities where health care services are woefully underdeveloped.

2023-01-07.abstract: Grocery stores have become a lightning rod for consumer discontent amid inflation and lingering mistrust after a bread price-fixing scandal.

2023-01-07.title: Bank Executive Accused of Urinating on a Fellow Airline Passenger
2023-01-07.abstract: The executive, for the Indian subsidiary of Wells Fargo, was fired after the investigation became public. Air India faces scrutiny for how it handled the matter.

2023-01-07.abstract: The sermon, a deeply symbolic moment after months of tension with the Moscow-led branch of the church, came as a cease-fire failed to materialize.

2023-01-08.abstract: As a crucial presidential election approaches, a newswoman’s resignation has raised a crucial question: Is her restrained approach also on its way out?

2023-01-08.abstract: Clubs in Khayelitsha, the nation’s second largest township, are attracting Black professionals with entertainment that “we love, we want.”

2023-01-09.abstract: Watch key moments of rioters attacking Brazil’s Congress, Presidential offices and the Supreme Court.

2023-01-10.abstract: The Chinese city is being gripped by a massive Covid wave, leading to a surge in hospitalizations and crowded funeral homes.

2023-01-11.abstract: Warm gestures by the American president have helped cement a critical relationship with a Mexican leader whose cooperation on migration, climate change and the drug trade is crucial.

2023-01-11.abstract: With a harsh winter putting sweeping offensives on hold, the war has shifted back to bloody battles of attrition like the one for the small city of Bakhmut and the smaller town of Soledar.

2023-01-11.abstract: An assailant injured six people with what French authorities described as a sharpened metal hook. The motive was not immediately clear.

2023-01-11.abstract: The virus spread to nine districts nationwide, infecting hundreds and killing dozens. Seven health workers were among those who died.

2023-01-13.abstract: Investigators want 23 people indicted but cleared senior authorities of fault in the Halloween tragedy. Victims’ families disputed the inquiry’s findings.

2023-01-15.abstract: A twin-engine propeller plane crashed on approach to the city of Pokhara, with 72 people on board.

2023-01-15.abstract: After Russia’s retreat from Kherson city, Ukrainians in other occupied areas of the south had expectations of quickly regaining their freedom. But those hopes have been dashed.

2023-01-18.abstract: The verdict was a rare legal victory for Ms. Ressa, founder of the news site Rappler, who has been under pressure from the Philippine authorities for years.

2023-01-18.abstract: Boris Pistorius, the new defense minister, is seen as a skilled politician but, entering a world stage on which Ukraine is at war, he may pose a risk for Chancellor Olaf Scholz.

2023-01-18.abstract: China struggled for years to curtail its rapid population growth. Now that its population is declining, economists and others fear serious implications for China and countries around the world.

2023-01-19.abstract: Ms. Ardern, a global liberal icon who has faced rising political headwinds at home, will leave office by Feb. 7, she said.

2023-01-19.abstract: For 1,000 years, homes dug into a desert cliff have sheltered olive farmers and sheep herders from summer heat and winter cold. But an exodus threatens its future. “We are left alone here.”

2023-01-19.abstract: New Zealand’s outgoing prime minister won global fame with youthful charisma and a frank, compassionate leadership style that carried her through crisis.

2023-01-20.abstract: As representatives from dozens of countries prepared to attend a war-planning meeting in Germany, the talk was of missiles, air defense and — above all — tanks.

2023-01-20.abstract: Ms. Ardern maneuvered through one crisis after another but had less success confronting persistent challenges that have hobbled successive governments.

2023-01-20.abstract: After the surprise had sunk in, speculation about her next move quickly began. The answer may lie in the priorities she professed before becoming prime minister.

2023-01-21.abstract: Insulting Islam or its founder is already a capital offense, but now those who insult people connected to the Prophet Muhammad could get prison time.

2023-01-21.abstract: While Germany has not yet made a decision on whether to send Leopard 2s to Ukraine, a Kyiv official said his country’s soldiers would begin training on them.

2023-01-22.abstract: With strict Covid restrictions lifted weeks ahead of the country’s most important holiday, millions, joyful yet anxious, could finally travel and reunite with family.

2023-01-22.abstract: Alexandre de Moraes, a Brazilian Supreme Court justice, was crucial to Brazil’s transfer of power. But his aggressive tactics are prompting debate: Can one go too far to fight the far right?

2023-01-22.abstract: A post-Nazi aversion to war and a commitment to promoting peace through engagement combines with an old fixation on Russia and a deep aversion to leading militarily.

2023-01-22.abstract: Aryeh Deri, who has a conviction for tax fraud, was deemed unfit to serve in the government, leaving Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in a legal and political predicament.

2023-01-23.abstract: For years, Beijing has thrown its wealth and weight across the globe. But its experience in the Solomon Islands calls into question its approach to expanding its power.

2023-01-23.abstract: The Ukrainian leader said the dismissal should serve as notice to officials whose actions “violate the principle of justice.”

2023-01-23.abstract: After months of punishing inflation and a plummeting currency, Egyptians are growing louder about the crisis. In exchange for a bailout, the I.M.F. is imposing stern conditions on the government.

2023-01-23.abstract: Amid Russia’s crackdown on resistance to the war in Ukraine, some have dared to lay bouquets and other offerings at a statue of a Ukrainian poet, protesting the recent Russian strike on civilians in Dnipro.

2023-01-24.abstract: Radio Free Europe/Radio Liberty, a news network originally set up as a C.I.A. operation early in the Cold War, is experiencing a renaissance and making a case for its independence.

2023-01-24.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2023-01-24.abstract: Seven killed in another mass shooting in California.

2023-01-24.title: They Poured Their Savings Into Homes That Were Never Built
2023-01-24.abstract: What is it like to pour your life savings into apartments that might never get built?

2023-01-24.title: A suspect is in custody after 7 people were killed in Half Moon Bay.
2023-01-24.abstract: The police arrested a 66-year-old suspect, who the county sheriff said was “fully cooperating.”

2023-01-24.title: Poland asks Germany’s permission to send its Leopard tanks to Ukraine.
2023-01-24.abstract: Germany says it will decide shortly on whether to send advanced tanks to Kyiv, which has been pleading for such equipment for months.

2023-01-25.title: Ukraine Fires Officials Amid Corruption Scandal, as Allies Watch Closely
2023-01-25.abstract: As it fights Russia’s invasion, Ukraine depends for its survival on aid from Western nations, which have concerns about endemic graft and how the money is used.

2023-01-25.title: Imagery Shows How a Cemetery for Russian Mercenaries Is Expanding
2023-01-25.abstract: Recent satellite imagery and video footage offer a rare look at combat fatalities sustained by the Wagner mercenary group in Ukraine.

2023-01-25.title: Getting tanks to the front lines looms as a challenge.
2023-01-25.abstract: The process of delivering Western weapons and other military equipment to Ukraine has been one of the most heavily guarded secrets of the war. Concerns that Russia will target roads, railways or staging grounds for the matériel as it is shipped to the front lines in eastern and southern Ukraine has required what officials and experts described as stealth convoys, usually cloaked in darkness or disguised, to evade attack.

2023-01-25.title: Inside a Nuclear War Bunker Built to Save Canada’s Leaders
2023-01-25.abstract: Amid renewed tensions with Russia, tourists are flocking to a decommissioned nuclear fallout shelter that Canada built to preserve its government during a nuclear war.

2023-01-25.title: The Spanish police make an arrest in the letter bomb case.
2023-01-25.abstract: A 74-year-old Spanish man was detained days after American and European officials said that Russian military intelligence officers might have directed the attacks.

2023-01-26.title: Germany and U.S., Dropping Resistance, Will Send Battle Tanks to Ukraine
2023-01-26.abstract: Although welcomed by Kyiv as a critical weapon to expel Russia’s invasion, the Leopard 2 tanks from Germany and Abrams tanks from the United States are unlikely to arrive soon.

2023-01-26.title: Ukraine War Accelerates Shift of Power in Europe to the East
2023-01-26.abstract: The war has raised the influence of Central and Eastern European countries with negative views of Russia, amplified calls to expand the E.U. and NATO, and dimmed the power of France and Germany.

2023-01-26.title: As Tough Elections Loom in Turkey, Erdogan Is Spending for Victory
2023-01-26.abstract: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan is trying to soften the blow of inflation on the population and using legal threats to bolster his position ahead of a vote that could reshape his country.

2023-01-26.title: Israeli Raid on West Bank City Kills Nine Palestinians, Officials Say
2023-01-26.abstract: The violence comes as Israel’s new far-right government has signaled even tougher actions against Palestinians. So far this year, 30 Palestinians have already been killed.

2023-01-26.title: North Korea Imposes Lockdown on Pyongyang to Fight ‘Recurrent Flu’
2023-01-26.abstract: A government notice made no mention of Covid-19, but state media said the authorities were taking measures in the capital to “cope with the daily-worsening world health crisis.”

2023-01-27.title: In Wave of Strikes, Russian Missiles Kill 12 Across Ukraine
2023-01-27.abstract: Russia’s forces once again pounded energy infrastructure and other civilian sites, a day after the United States and Germany pledged to send dozens of tanks to Ukraine.

2023-01-27.title: Is Australia Day Approaching a Tipping Point?
2023-01-27.abstract: A majority of Australian adults under 35 support changing the date from Jan. 26.

2023-01-27.title: Will U.K. Rejection of Scottish Gender Bill Bolster Independence Movement?
2023-01-27.abstract: The intervention from London angered Scottish politicians, but it’s not clear that backers of independence can use the issue to their advantage.

2023-01-27.title: Teenage Rapper, Rooted in Mapuche Identity, Roars for Indigenous Rights
2023-01-27.abstract: MC Millaray, 16, an emerging music star in Chile, uses her fierce lyrics to convey five centuries of struggles by the country’s largest Indigenous group against European colonizers.

2023-01-27.title: Asia Has Had a Really Cold Month, Courtesy of the Polar Vortex
2023-01-27.abstract: An exceptionally cold January has brought the region misery and snarled travel. Experts blame the same arctic system that hit the United States last month.

2023-01-28.title: Russia Outlaws a News Site and a Rights Group, Stifling Critical Voices
2023-01-28.abstract: The Kremlin stepped up efforts to banish independent sources of news. Prosecutors labeled Meduza, a popular news site, “undesirable,” so anyone who aids or promotes it can be prosecuted.

2023-01-28.title: New Zealand’s Largest City Grapples With Aftermath of Devastating Floods
2023-01-28.abstract: At least three people were killed in the flooding in Auckland that forced hundreds of people from their homes and shut down the city’s airport, which was submerged.

2023-01-28.title: Kharkiv Got Some Breathing Space, but Still Doesn’t Breathe Easily
2023-01-28.abstract: For the past four months, residents have slowly trickled back into the reclaimed city. But signs of the conflict — and the chance that it might return — are everywhere.

2023-01-28.title: What Happened to Canada’s Cold War Relics?
2023-01-28.abstract: Few of the sites built to protect Canadians from a radioactive cloud of destruction have been preserved.

2023-01-28.title: 2 Israelis Injured in Jerusalem Attack, Hours After a Nearby Mass Shooting
2023-01-28.abstract: Two Israelis were shot and injured on Saturday in East Jerusalem, the day after an attack outside a synagogue elsewhere in the city killed seven people.

2023-01-29.title: Iran Says It Thwarted a Drone Attack on a Munitions Facility
2023-01-29.abstract: The government did not say who was behind the attempted attack, but some Telegram channels affiliated with Iran’s Revolutionary Guards pointed the finger at Israel.

2023-01-29.title: Bus Plunge in Pakistan Kills at Least 40
2023-01-29.abstract: The vehicle plunged down a ravine while traveling more than 300 miles from Quetta to the port city of Karachi.

2023-01-29.title: Palestinian Man Fatally Shot as Violence Continues in Israel
2023-01-29.abstract: Tensions and violence have gripped the Israeli-occupied West Bank and Jerusalem for days after an Israeli military raid on Thursday killed 10 people.

2023-01-29.title: U.K. Prime Minister Ousts Top Party Official
2023-01-29.abstract: In trying to bring “integrity” back to Downing Street, Rishi Sunak fired Nadhim Zahawi, the chairman of the governing Conservatives, over his personal tax affairs.

2023-01-29.title: Russia and Ukraine Battle for Control of Villages Near the Key City of Bakhmut
2023-01-29.abstract: The area is a flash point in an offensive that Moscow views as crucial for its goal of seizing the Donbas region of eastern Ukraine.

2023-01-30.title: Israel Launched Drone Attack on Iranian Facility, Officials Say
2023-01-30.abstract: While the target’s purpose is unclear, the city of Isfahan is a major center of Iranian missile production, research and development.

2023-01-30.title: ‘Squid Game’ Actor Faces Trial on Sex Crime Charges This Week
2023-01-30.abstract: Oh Young-soo, the actor who portrayed a contestant in the popular Netflix series, is accused of inappropriately touching a South Korean woman in 2017.

2023-01-30.title: Gandhi’s Life in Photos, 75 Years After His Assassination
2023-01-30.abstract: Known as the father of Indian independence, his concept of nonviolent resistance to fight injustice has inspired political movements around the world.

2023-01-30.title: Russia Freed Prisoners to Fight Its War. Here’s How Some Fared.
2023-01-30.abstract: Tens of thousands of inmates have joined a mercenary group fighting with the Kremlin’s decimated forces in Ukraine. Some of them are returning to civilian life with military training and, in many cases, battlefield traumas.

2023-01-30.title: Baghdad Loses Green Space to Real Estate Boom
2023-01-30.abstract: The problem is driving up temperatures in what is already one of the hottest cities in the world, where air-conditioning is a luxury only the rich can afford.

2023-01-31.title: Ukraine Urges Allies to Speed Up Delivery of Weaponry
2023-01-31.abstract: “We have to make time our weapon,” President Volodymyr Zelensky declared as Ukrainians girded for an expected Russian offensive.

2023-01-31.title: With tanks in the pipeline, Ukraine redoubles its efforts to obtain warplanes.
2023-01-31.abstract: Ukraine’s allies have supplied Kyiv with an ever-growing list of weaponry, but many countries are wary of its recent requests for military jets.

2023-01-31.title: A Risky Trade in Ukraine Grows Riskier Amid the War
2023-01-31.abstract: Russia’s invasion has disrupted the social services that help reduce harm to the women and men who sell sex, threatening public health.

2023-01-31.title: Blinken Meets With Palestinian Leader After Surge in Violence
2023-01-31.abstract: Following talks with Israeli officials in Jerusalem, the U.S. secretary of state, Antony J. Blinken, met the Palestinian leadership in the occupied West Bank city of Ramallah amid a sharp rise in tensions in the region.

2023-01-31.title: Russia’s economic growth suggests Western sanctions are having a limited impact.
2023-01-31.abstract: The resilience of Russia’s economy is helping fuel global growth, according to a new report by the International Monetary Fund, suggesting that efforts by Western nations to weaken Moscow because of its war in Ukraine appear to be faltering.
